subject: Product Management
title: Slack's Accidental Fit
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Slack's Accidental Fit.

hook.text: Slack was never supposed to be a product. It was an internal chat tool built by a failing game company.

buildup.text: Tiny Speck was making a game called Glitch. They built an internal messaging tool to coordinate their remote team.

discovery.text: Glitch failed. But the team noticed something: they loved the chat tool more than the game they were building.

twist.text: Stewart Butterfield pivoted the entire company. He released the chat tool to other teams and asked for feedback.

climax.text: Slack hit PMF so hard it became the fastest-growing business app in history — $1 billion in ARR in record time.

punchline.text: The best product was the one they built for themselves.

quiz.question: How did Slack find product-market fit?
options: It was an internal tool for a failed game company that users loved more than the game
It was carefully planned from the beginning
It copied an existing messaging product
